transcript: Speaker 1: Thanks for joining. Let's discuss the Q3 pipeline for Acme Corp. Speaker 2: John Smith here with Sarah Chen from procurement. Speaker 1: We approved the Q3 budget of £50k and move from proposal to negotiation. Speaker 2: Sarah will send the revised proposal by Friday. Speaker 1: Next step is a follow-up call next Tuesday.
expected_summary: Discussed Q3 pipeline, budget approval, and proposal timing.

transcript: Speaker 1: Good morning — Contoso renewal. Speaker 2: We are moving from qualification to proposal after today's scoping. Speaker 1: Decision: proceed with technical review. Speaker 2: Alex will schedule the workshop.

Speaker 1: Demo completed; economic buyer wants pricing. Speaker 2: We shift negotiation to closed won once MSAs are signed. Speaker 1: Agreed — deal is £95k. Speaker 2: Legal will circulate templates Monday.

Speaker 1: Pipeline review — Northwind wants a pilot. Speaker 2: Stage moves from lead to qualification after discovery call. Speaker 1: Next steps: send security questionnaire. Speaker 2: Chris owns it.

Speaker 1: We need executive sign-off on the SOW. Speaker 2: Moving proposal to negotiation after pricing acceptance. Speaker 1: Taylor will consolidate redlines. Speaker 2: Decision: cap professional services at 200 hours.

Speaker 1: Competitive deal — we matched the rival quote. Speaker 2: Stage stays in negotiation until procurement counters. Speaker 1: Sam will share comparison sheet. Speaker 2: Positive tone overall.
Speaker 1: Yesterday: API merge. Today: tests. Blocker: flaky CI. Speaker 2: I'll fix the runner today.
Speaker 1: Design blocked on brand assets. Speaker 2: I'll upload by noon. Speaker 1: Update: dashboard metrics shipped.
Speaker 1: Quick standup — no blockers; releasing hotfix. Speaker 2: Same.
Speaker 1: Blocked on legal review of vendor terms. Speaker 2: I'll escalate. Speaker 1: Risk noted.

Speaker 1: Welcome to the client kickoff for Project Aurora. Speaker 2: Attendees: Alice, Bob, Carol, Dan, Erin, Frank. Speaker 1: Action: Alice owns RACI; Bob owns timeline; Carol owns integrations. Speaker 2: Dan will set up Slack; Erin will schedule weekly demos; Frank will security review.
Speaker 1: Implementation kickoff — team: Grace, Henry, Ivy, Jack. Speaker 1: Grace runs training; Henry data migration; Ivy UAT; Jack go-live. Speaker 2: Decision: training week starts May 5.
Speaker 1: Product kickoff with Kelly, Leo, Mona, Nina, Omar. Speaker 2: Kelly: PRD; Leo: API; Mona: UX; Nina: analytics; Omar: rollout. Speaker 1: Next: weekly design reviews.
Speaker 1: Partner kickoff — Paula, Quinn, Rosa, Steve. Speaker 1: Paula contract; Quinn integrations; Rosa support; Steve billing. Speaker 2: Decision: pilot starts April 10.
Speaker 1: Quarterly business review — revenue beat plan. Speaker 2: Decision: reinvest £20k into demand gen. Speaker 1: Follow-up: finance model refresh next week. Sentiment: positive.
Speaker 1: Architecture review concluded. Speaker 2: Decision: adopt event bus. Speaker 1: Follow-ups: write ADR and schedule migration. Neutral sentiment.
Speaker 1: Sprint review — velocity dipped. Speaker 2: Decision: cut scope for release. Speaker 1: Follow-up: reprioritize backlog. Sentiment: slightly negative.
Speaker 1: One-on-one — career growth. Speaker 2: I'd like more leadership scope. Speaker 1: Action: take ownership of onboarding initiative. Next: check-in in 2 weeks.
Speaker 1: Performance check-in. Speaker 2: I need clearer goals. Speaker 1: Action: draft OKRs together by Friday. Personal follow-up scheduled.
Speaker 1: Wellbeing one-on-one. Speaker 2: Workload feels high. Speaker 1: Action: redistribute one project. Next: lighter sprint next cycle.
Speaker 1: No actions today — informational only.
